prompt: Write a 150-word short story about a lighthouse keeper who discovers something unexpected in the fog. Do not use dialogue.
response:
The old lighthouse keeper trudge up the spiral stairs, his joints creaking with each step. Years of tending the flame had left him weathered, both inside and out. As he reached the top, a thick fog engulfed the lantern room. He frowned, squinting into the mist.

Suddenly, a strange shape materialized - a figure standing in the center of the room. The keeper froze. The figure seemed to shimmer, its edges dissolving into the fog. It was glowing, casting an ethereal blue light.

With a jolt, the keeper recognized the luminous form. It was his wife, who had passed away years ago. Tears welled up as memories flooded back. He reached out a trembling hand, longing to caress her face, but she was gone in a wisp, disappearing into the fog.

The keeper stood there, transfixed, until the fog lifted. He knew he had witnessed something miraculous, a fleeting connection to the other side. With a newfound purpose, he struck the match to light the wick, his heart filled with a warmth that transcended the physical world.
